_README: PERMANENT Wayond certification corpus. REAL observed messages ONLY — never synthetic (GFX-PKT-WAYOND-CORPUS-SEED-FROM-SCREENSHOTS). V1 extracted from Nuno's Telegram screenshots (channel 'Wayond | FX Signals', 24 Jun–02 Jul 2026). Each entry Nuno supplies becomes a fixed regression case. expected_type is the human-certified ground truth; the framework asserts the pipeline routes it safely (only ENTRY_SIGNAL may become tradeable). meta flags: is_edit / media / is_reply / stale. NOTE: many TP-hit updates carry an MT5 position screenshot; classified by their TEXT here (media handling is a dispatcher concern) — flagged per entry. Prices transcribed verbatim from the screenshots; no prices guessed.

messages.notes: Real SELL entry. IMPORTANT format divergence: SELL messages use 'STOP LOSS:' and 'TP1:' with COLONS (BUY messages do not). The original parser's 'Stop Loss\s+<num>' misses this -> parser gap fixed in this packet.

messages.notes: A BUY ENTRY that arrived EDITED ('8 edited 10:37'). Ratified WAYOND-EDIT-MEDIA policy (PR #72): an edited entry is NOT dropped — it is surfaced to the human-approval gate FLAGGED (PendingSignalApproval.source_edited=true) so the reviewer verifies entry/SL/TP. Still human-gated (never auto-traded), so its routing taxonomy is ENTRY_SIGNAL.

messages.text:
TP1 HIT! That gives us +23 pips.

Move SL to BE
messages.notes: TP1-hit (caps 'HIT') + 'Move SL to BE' (break-even, non-numeric) — reply to AUDUSD BUY 0.68920, with a position screenshot. Recognised via the TP1-HIT token; 'Move SL to BE' has no numeric target so the move-SL regex does not fire (informational). Media = evidence; parsed from text → UPDATE.
